symptoms.symptom_name: Right-upper-quadrant discomfort beginning immediately after an abnormal-LFT alert

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symptom onset tied precisely to an anxiety-provoking message strongly suggests a functional/somatic mechanism.
RUQ location is compatible with biliary disease but the mild vague quality and psychological trigger are atypical for it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild (3/10), intermittent pain that eases with distraction

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute cholecystitis typically causes severe, sustained RUQ pain; mild intermittent pain relieved by distraction argues against it.
Fluctuation with attention and distraction is characteristic of functional/somatic pain.
Nausea is non-specific; the absence of vomiting reduces suspicion of an obstructive biliary process.
No fever/rigors makes cholangitis less likely (breaks Charcot's triad), but self-report without a thermometer does not objectively exclude it.
No jaundice reduces suspicion of biliary obstruction; early scleral icterus is, however, easily missed on self-inspection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of encephalopathy/coagulopathy (clear thinking, no bruising/bleeding)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal mentation and no bleeding make acute liver failure very unlikely, though these are self-reported.
A strictly negative Murphy's sign substantially lowers the probability of acute cholecystitis — but it is a clinician examination the AI Doctor cannot perform, so it stays sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-distended abdomen; no hepatomegaly/splenomegaly; afebrile; no peripheral stigmata of chronic liver disease

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A benign abdomen with only vague deep tenderness and no guarding argues strongly against an acute surgical abdomen — a sealed clinician measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated liver transaminases on the pathology alert (mild transaminitis)

loinc_ref.display: Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma

investigation_details.expected_result: Mildly elevated transaminases consistent with chronic low-grade autoimmune interface hepatitis; bilirubin and synthetic function expected normal.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot distinguish a chronic stable transaminitis from an acute hepatitic or obstructive rise.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Somatic/anxiety-amplified discomfort on known mild liver disease
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A mild transaminitis matching the known chronic pattern (rather than a new large rise or bilirubin spike) supports a stable chronic picture — but the raw values are lab-derived and sealed; only the patient's vague 'abnormal bloods' report reaches the AI.

symptoms.symptom_name: No hepatotoxic medication, paracetamol overuse or recent alcohol excess

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No paracetamol/hepatotoxin exposure and negligible alcohol reduce the likelihood of an acute toxic hepatitic rise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked health anxiety with hypervigilant self-palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Repeated self-palpation causes local tissue irritation and heightened visceral awareness, directly generating and maintaining the discomfort.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive SMA/ANA and prior mild interface hepatitis on histology

investigation_details.investigation_name: Autoantibody panel (SMA, ANA) and prior liver histology
investigation_details.expected_result: Positive smooth-muscle and antinuclear antibodies with mild histological interface hepatitis — the established basis of the known diagnosis.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without the historical serology/histology the chronic autoimmune basis is not established; this is prior sealed record data unavailable to the telehealth AI.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positive SMA/ANA with interface hepatitis is the diagnostic substrate of autoimmune hepatitis — historical, record-based, and sealed.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The absence of fever, jaundice and severe pain breaks Charcot's triad and lowers cholangitis probability — but each component here is patient-reported, not objectively measured.
Onset tied to an alarming message, mild pain fluctuating with attention, and hypervigilant self-palpation together point to a functional mechanism.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person abdominal examination and vital signs (including temperature)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single highest-value next step: examination (Murphy's, guarding, hepatomegaly) and measured vitals discriminate a benign somatic picture from acute cholecystitis/cholangitis — none obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat/comprehensive liver function tests with bilirubin and coagulation profile
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Distinguishes a stable chronic transaminitis from an acute hepatitic or obstructive rise and assesses synthetic function (failure risk).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Autoantibody panel (SMA/ANA) and outpatient hepatology review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms/monitors the chronic autoimmune basis; appropriate in the outpatient setting, not acutely.
First-line imaging if biliary pathology is suspected after examination; arranged through the in-person service.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT abdomen / broad emergency imaging on telehealth suspicion alone
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Over-investigation driven by anxiety/alarm; imaging should follow in-person clinical assessment, not precede it, in a well patient with mild pain.
